steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Use the rule $\sqrt{x} \times \sqrt{y} = \sqrt{xy}$ to combine the two radicals: $\sqrt{(a+c)^3} \times \sqrt{a+c} = \sqrt{(a+c)^3 \cdot (a+c)} = \sqrt{(a+c)^4}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• '$a + c$': This would require the expression to equal $(a+c)^1$, but we derived $(a+c)^2$. ✗
  • '$a^2 + c^2$': Missing the cross term $2ac$; this is $(a+c)^2$ only if $ac=0$, which is impossible since both are positive. ✗
  • '$a^2 + 2a c + c^2$': Exactly the expansion of $(a+c)^2$. ✓
  • '$a^2 \times c^2$': This equals $(ac)^2$, a product, which has no relation to $(a+c)^2$. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
